summary: Commodity AUDIT register from Incahuasi Sector C UE 2 -- the audit partner of UR255 (day-book). 176 cords (118 L1), 24 clusters, AB-dominant (81%). v3 unlocks 14 vocabulary occurrences (7 unique: chiqa x5, qaqa x3, chaqa x2, kaki x1, kaqa x1, taqa x1, maqa x1) where v1 saw only 3 STRING. STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE: 10-constant on 23 of 118 L1 cords (19.5%) at ~3-cord cadence, chiqa x5 dominance (corpus audit-verification word), 8/10 Ascher sums verified. The doubled chiqa seal convention (p91+p92, p116+p117) and chaqa-doubled delivery confirmation (p107+p108) are IDENTICAL to UR267B's seal pattern -- the STANDARDIZED INCA AUDIT FORMAT. Together with UR255 (day-book, 15-constant, 1/6 Ascher), UR256 completes the Sector C aji audit pair, structurally parallel to UR267A+UR267B at Sector A UE 16.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0492 = UR256. 176 cords (118 L1 + 58 subsidiaries to L4). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema STRUCTURED depth L4.
  24 clusters, averaging 7.3 cords each.
  3 colors: AB(143, 81%), MB(32, 18%), KB(1). Monochromatic AB palette with MB punctuation -- highest single-color concentration of the Incahuasi set analyzed.
  Excavation: Sector C, Subsector 5, Unidad 19K, UE 2. 'Big pile' per OKR notes. DIFFERENT SECTOR AND STOREROOM from UR267A/B/UR278 (Sector A UE 16) and from UR268/UR270 (Sector A UE 11/13/18).
  OKR note: 'UR 267-B Match'. Match is STRUCTURAL (same 10-constant signature, same audit-profile), not same-commodity or same-pipeline.
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); not individually discussed in Urton (2017).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED ~12, KFG_RESOLVED ~49, OKR_ONLY ~28, DIVERGENT 1, KFG_ONLY ~3.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.19 (low; 47 L? cords the merge now fills).
  v1 -> v3: 3 -> 14 STRING readings (+11), 1 -> 7 distinct vocabulary (+6), 49 L? filled.
  Old v1 contribution called this '3 STRING (all qaqa)' and proposed a Sector C production-register role; v3 reveals an audit register with 7-word vocabulary.

ASCHER LAYER
  11 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 3 (Merkle).
  covering_ratio: modest. Capacity class: LOW.
  Verified checksums: 8 / 10 = 80%. Second-best integrity rate of the Incahuasi set (only UR267B with 11/11 beats it). UR256's arithmetic is clean.
  Pass 3A: 0. Pass 3C: 1 propagated. Pass 3D: 0.
  Interpretation: UR256 is an AUDIT-GRADE document whose sum structure arithmetically holds in most cases. Consistent with UR267B-style role.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- THE '10' CONSTANT
  23 cords carry EXACTLY the value 10 (S10).
  Positions: p3, p11, p17, p23, p29, p35, p41, p46, p53, p62, p70, p74, p78, p85, p90, p93, p96, p100, p103, p106, p109, p115, p118.
  Spacing: modal 3-6 cords (some 7-8 when empties intervene).
  Colors: 21/23 on AB, 2 on other.
  SAME '10 constant' regime as UR267B -- positional audit-tick every ~3-6 cords. UR256 density 23/118 = 19.5%, UR267B density 26/103 = 25%. Compatible structural regime; slightly lower density in UR256.

VOCABULARY (7 words, 14 occurrences, 100% dict hits)
  chiqa x5 (truth / verification seal -- the AUDIT VERB)
  qaqa x3 (rock-landmark)
  chaqa x2 (delivery / transfer across boundary)
  kaki x1 (dried corn in commodity context -- NOT Pleiades)
  kaqa x1 (terrace wall)
  taqa x1 (boundary marker)
  maqa x1 (account-mark posted)
  Cadastral audit signature: 5 of 8 canonical markers (qaqa, kaqa, taqa, chaqa, chiqa). The kaki reading (dried corn) is the commodity-context gloss overriding the astronomical Pleiades meaning.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p26 kaki (AB, dried corn entry -- the only kaki in the document)
  p36 taqa (AB, boundary marker)
  p43 maqa (MB, account-mark, on the phase-marker color)
  p63 kaqa (AB, wall marker)
  p65 qaqa (AB, rock landmark)
  p91+p92 chiqa chiqa (AB, DOUBLED verification -- first audit seal)
  p95 chiqa (AB)
  p101 qaqa (AB)
  p107+p108 chaqa chaqa (AB, DOUBLED delivery -- same doubled pattern as UR267B p89+p91)
  p116+p117 chiqa chiqa (AB, DOUBLED verification -- terminal audit seal)
  The two doubled-chiqa pairs (p91-p92 and p116-p117) are formal SEAL stamps, identical to the UR267B practice.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (143, 81%) = PRIMARY DATA + vocabulary carrier. All vocabulary lives on AB except maqa at p43.
  MB (32, 18%) = PHASE-MARKER channel. p43 maqa on MB is the one mid-document phase-pivot cord.
  KB (1) = rare, function unclear.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR256 opens with a massive value 1055 (p1, AB) -- the first aggregate being audited. Two cords later (p3) the first '10' tick is set. The document then unrolls a 118-cord audit rhythm. First vocabulary appears at p26 as kaki (dried corn entry), then taqa (boundary) at p36, maqa (account-mark) at p43. The middle section is numerical. The AUDIT SEALS appear in the final third: chiqa chiqa at p91-p92 (first formal verification stamp), chiqa alone at p95, chaqa chaqa at p107-p108 (delivery confirmed twice), chiqa chiqa at p116-p117 (terminal verification). This is the structural twin of UR267B's two-stage doubled-chiqa seal pattern. UR256 is a PARALLEL AUDIT register from Sector C UE 2, possibly auditing a different commodity (kaki = dried corn rather than aji specifically?) or a different production zone than the UR267A+UR267B aji pipeline. The 'UR 267-B Match' OKR note refers to this STRUCTURAL resemblance, not a content or pipeline link.
  UR256 is the audit partner of UR255 (the 15-constant day-book physically wrapped around it in the same 'Big pile').

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  The doubled-chiqa seal convention (two adjacent chiqa cords framing a verified block) is a STANDARDIZED INCA AUDIT FORMAT attested on both UR256 (Sector C UE 2) and UR267B (Sector A UE 16). Two independent occurrences at separate sectors of Incahuasi confirms the convention as the empire's canonical audit closure.
  The commodity-context polysemy resolves kaki = dried corn (not Pleiades) because (a) provenance is a storeroom not an observatory, (b) no supporting astronomical markers appear (no mama, paka, maki, chaki), (c) taqa and kaqa are cadastral markers that anchor the document on the ground.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 AB val=1055: opening aggregate being audited.
  p3 AB val=10: FIRST '10' -- audit-tick convention established.
  p26 AB kaki: first vocabulary -- dried corn entry (commodity, NOT Pleiades).
  p43 MB maqa: account-mark on the phase-marker color -- mid-document pivot.
  p91+p92 chiqa chiqa: FIRST DOUBLED SEAL.
  p107+p108 chaqa chaqa: DOUBLED delivery confirmation.
  p116+p117 chiqa chiqa: TERMINAL DOUBLED SEAL.
  p118 val=10: LAST '10' -- audit closes.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  8/10 Ascher verification: programmatically reproducible.
  23 '10' cord positions: reproducible.
  Doubled-chiqa seal pattern (p91/p92, p116/p117): reproducible.
  Doubled-chaqa delivery (p107/p108): reproducible.
  kaki = dried corn polysemy: context-check against any supporting astronomical vocabulary (expected absent).
  Physical 'Big pile' context: confirm UR256 and UR255 are in the same storage bundle.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - '3 STRING (all qaqa), Sector C production register' -- REVERSED: v3 gives 14 readings / 7 words including chiqa x5 + doubled-seal convention.
  - Classification 'Sector C production register' -- REFINED to 'Sector C aji audit', paired with UR255.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 176 cords, AB-dominant, Incahuasi Sector C UE 2.
  - 'Big pile' storage context.
  - Structural match with UR267B (preserved from OKR; now quantified with 10-constant + doubled seals).
  - Commodity-accounting classification (now sharpened to audit tier).
narrative:
A thousand and fifty-five on light brown. That is the first cord. Something was counted, and it came to one thousand and fifty-five. Then one empty cord. Then ten.

The ten returns every three or four cords for the next hundred. On light brown, always. The scribe is auditing -- deducting an audit-tick of ten, over and over, the same gesture as the keeper of UR267B in Sector A one hundred metres away. Different sector, different storeroom, same Inca administrative grammar.

At cord twenty-six the scribe writes his first word: kaki. In astronomical context this would be the Pleiades. But this is not an astronomical khipu -- the cord's neighbors are numerical aggregates, the color is the same commodity-light-brown as all 143 cords in AB, and kaki here reads in its other sense: dried corn. The audit knows what it is counting.

At cord thirty-six, taqa -- boundary. At cord forty-three, on the one medium-brown cord that interrupts the light-brown sequence, maqa: the mark is posted. The MB cord is acting as a phase-marker, and the scribe inscribes his structural pivot on it with a single-stroke verb.

The middle of the document is pure number. Cords fifty through ninety carry the running totals of what is being audited -- 523, 526, 623, 610, 601, 580, 535, 525 on light brown, each separated by its '10' tick. At cord sixty-three, a brief vocabulary breath: kaqa, the terrace wall. At sixty-five, qaqa, the rock. These anchor the commodity to its producing field.

Then the audit phase begins. At cords ninety-one and ninety-two the scribe writes chiqa twice on light brown. Verified, verified. This is the first formal seal. At ninety-five, a single chiqa -- a minor re-verification. At one hundred and one, qaqa again -- the second landmark call-out, marking the audited block. At one hundred and seven and one hundred and eight, chaqa chaqa: delivered, delivered. The deliveries in the audited range are confirmed to have crossed the storeroom threshold.

The final seal is at one hundred and sixteen and one hundred and seventeen: chiqa chiqa on light brown. Verified, verified. The last cord is one hundred and eighteen: ten. One more audit tick. Document closed.

Eight of the ten Ascher pendant-pendant sums verify arithmetically five centuries later -- the same good-integrity rate as UR267B's eleven of eleven. Two different scribes, two different sectors of Incahuasi, two different commodities, one shared Inca administrative craft.

prior_work.claim: The 10-constant is a commodity-audit positional signature at Incahuasi (published).
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse. Latin American Antiquity 26(4).

prior_work.claim: UR256's OKR note flags 'UR 267-B Match' -- structural resemblance between the two audit documents at separate Incahuasi sectors.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-note for UR256 (Urton).

prior_work.claim: The kaki polysemy (Pleiades AND dried corn / Qollqa granary) is standard Quechua linguistics: same root names both the sky object and the ground category (calendar-agricultural function).
prior_work.citation: Bertonio 1612; Gonzalez Holguin 1608; Zuidema and Bauer-Dearborn 1995 on Pleiades-Qollqa calendar marker.

v3_contribution.claim: 8/10 Ascher verified (80 percent) -- the second-best integrity rate in the analysed Incahuasi set (only UR267B at 100 percent exceeds it). Confirms UR256 as an audit-grade document whose arithmetic holds under modern machine parsing.

Doubled-chiqa seal convention at p91+p92 and p116+p117, and doubled-chaqa delivery at p107+p108 -- IDENTICAL to UR267B's seal pattern. Evidence that the doubled-seal scribal convention is a STANDARDIZED Inca audit format, not a single-scribe quirk.

evidence.test: Doubled-vocabulary scribal seal at cord level: chiqa chiqa, chaqa chaqa

v3_contribution.claim: Positional map of 23 '10' cords at ~3-6 cord cadence (density 19.5 percent of L1). Structural parallel to UR267B's 10-constant at 25 percent density -- same regime, compatible spacing, different sector.

evidence.test: Positional constant count at value=10 on L1

v3_contribution.claim: kaki at p26 read as 'dried corn' (commodity context) rather than 'Pleiades' (astronomical) -- the sky/ground polysemy rule applied to a monochromatic storeroom document with no supporting astronomical markers.
v3 merged corpus unlocks 14 STRING readings (v1 had 3), filling 49 L? cords. Seven-word audit vocabulary (chiqa x5 dominant) reveals what v1 saw as 'three qaqa' is a full audit register.

evidence.test: Dictionary-confirmed STRING readings after KFG merge

companions.note: UR256 has UR255's beginning cord physically wrapped around it (Urton's Big Pile note). UR255 is the tier-1 day-book with 15-constant, 1/6 Ascher verified; UR256 audits it with 10-constant, 8/10 verified. Together they form the Sector C aji (or dried-corn) day-book+audit pair.

evidence.test: OKR field-note attestation of origin binding

companions.note: Sector A UE 16 audit partner with IDENTICAL seal convention: doubled chiqa at p74+p76, p83+p85 and doubled chaqa at p89+p91. UR256 mirrors this pattern at p91+p92, p107+p108, p116+p117. Two independent audit documents at separate sectors with the same scribal format -- a STANDARDIZED Inca audit template.

evidence.test: Doubled-chiqa + doubled-chaqa seal pattern match across UR256 and UR267B

companions.note: Sector A day-book whose 15-constant / low-Ascher profile parallels UR255's. UR256 audits UR255 in Sector C as UR267B audits UR267A in Sector A.

UE 16 commodity production matrix. UR278 demonstrates the sky/ground polysemy (kaki = Pleiades AND Qollqa/granary) whose ground-reading applies to UR256's kaki at p26 as 'dried corn' rather than astronomical Pleiades.

word_reading.note: 14/14 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent hit rate). Seven-word audit vocabulary (chiqa x5 dominant). kaki polysemy resolved (dried corn, not Pleiades) via context. No Pass 3A/3D needed.

document_type.note: Commodity_accounting audit tier anchored by (a) 10-constant positional signature at 19.5 percent density, (b) chiqa x5 corpus-high audit-verb density, (c) doubled-chiqa and doubled-chaqa seals matching UR267B's format, (d) 8/10 Ascher verified. Strong convergent evidence.

narrative.note: Internally coherent (opening aggregate -> 10-tick rhythm -> vocabulary bloom p26-p65 -> doubled-chiqa seals p91-p92 -> doubled-chaqa delivery p107-p108 -> terminal doubled chiqa p116-p117 -> closure at p118). Externally corroborated by the UR267B structural match and by the sky/ground polysemy framework. The specific commodity being audited (dried corn? aji? mixed?) is underdetermined -- kaki at p26 suggests dried corn, but only one occurrence.

data_integrity.note: 8/10 Ascher sums verified (80 percent -- second-highest in the analysed Incahuasi set). OKR/KFG agreement 0.19 is low in raw terms because v1 had 49 L? cords; post-merge 1 DIVERGENT cord (under 10 percent threshold). Audit-grade integrity.

sections.title: Opening Aggregate + Audit Ticks
sections.reading: p1 carries 1055 (the first aggregate being audited, probably pulled from UR255). p3 opens the '10' audit-tick rhythm that continues every 3-6 cords for the rest of the document. No vocabulary yet.

sections.title: First Vocabulary + Mid-Pivot
sections.reading: kaki (dried corn, commodity context) at p26 opens the verbal register. taqa (boundary) at p36. At p43 the scribe switches to the MB phase-marker color for a single cord carrying maqa -- the mid-document structural pivot. Returns to AB for kaqa at p63 and qaqa at p65.

key_cords.note: FIRST vocabulary -- dried corn (NOT Pleiades, sky/ground polysemy rule)
PHASE-MARKER COLOR SHIFT -- mid-document pivot, account-mark posted

sections.title: First Audit Seal
sections.reading: The audit verification opens: CHIQA CHIQA at p91+p92 (DOUBLED SEAL -- the first formal audit stamp). Follow-up single chiqa at p95 re-verifies. qaqa at p101 re-anchors to the rock-landmark closing the first audited block.

key_cords.note: DOUBLED SEAL opening -- first formal verification
rock-landmark closing the first audited block

sections.title: Delivery Confirmation
sections.reading: CHAQA CHAQA at p107+p108 -- two adjacent delivery-verbs confirming two consignments crossed the storeroom threshold. Same doubled convention as UR267B's p89+p91.

key_cords.note: DOUBLED DELIVERY opening -- first consignment confirmed
DOUBLED DELIVERY closing -- second consignment confirmed

sections.title: Terminal Seal + Closure
sections.reading: CHIQA CHIQA at p116+p117 -- terminal doubled verification seal stamps the whole ledger. p118 carries the LAST '10' audit-tick. The audit is closed.

key_cords.note: TERMINAL DOUBLED SEAL closing -- audit stamped complete
